codeReview.summary: The context-pipeline feature provides a complete tiered context assembly pipeline with proper token budget enforcement, condensation levels, and auto-compaction. Implementation is idiomatic Rust with good test coverage (17 tests). No blocking issues found.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used `chrono::DateTime` and `chrono::Duration` in TierBuilder::with_conversation for timestamp-based priority calculation. The AGENTS.md Rust patterns section does not mention that chrono is available as a workspace dependency. This could help future workers avoid guessing about available time/date libraries.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: context_pipeline.rs:434-452 uses chrono for age_hours calculation. chrono.workspace = true in Cargo.toml

sharedStateObservations.observation: The context_pipeline module is designed as a local assembler that receives pre-built PipelineContextItems from other systems (knowledge graph, vector search). The transcript shows it integrates with context_chunking.rs which converts AST chunks to context items. The feature's preconditions (knowledge graph complete, vector search complete) were satisfied by prior features. This design pattern - a local assembler receiving items from external providers - is not explicitly documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: context_chunking.rs uses ContextAssembler via chunks_to_context_items(). The assembler takes Vec<PipelineContextItem> rather than directly calling external systems.

summary: context-pipeline feature passes review. The implementation provides a complete tiered context assembly pipeline with 5 priority tiers (ActiveFile, OpenRecent, VectorSearch, GraphExpanded, ConversationHistory), priority-based sorting, budget enforcement with truncation to warning_threshold (65%), condensation level detection (Ok/Warning/MustCondense), and auto-compaction toggle. All 17 unit tests pass. The feature integrates with context_chunking.rs and satisfies its feature contract. No blocking issues found.
